# PAGE CONTEXT: about.md

## Page Voice Note

This is the credibility deep-dive for visitors already interested. They've scrolled past the homepage. They're now evaluating whether to trust WebXC with their business. The About page is where founder stories, principal expertise, and team depth land. Visitors here are already considering — reward their attention with specifics, warmth, and reassurance.

**Voice calibration:** Warmer and more narrative than homepage, but still authoritative. This page answers the unspoken question: "Who are these people and can I trust them?" The tone should be confident but approachable. Show personality without losing authority. Emphasise accountability, hands-on leadership, and the partnership model (not the vendor model). Visitors here are often Burned Decision Makers or Medical/YMYL specialists — people for whom trust is non-negotiable. Every detail about how James and Andy work together should reinforce that they do the work themselves, not delegate to juniors.

## Relevant Source Files

- Service-Offerings.md (principal-led approach, methodology, service delivery)
- Positioning.md (principal differentiator, value themes, audience personas)
- Key Messaging.md (core message, benefit proofs)
- Writer Prompt (trust signals, principal visibility, brand voice)
- Writing Style Guide (sentence rhythm, bilingual voice, peer-to-peer tone)

## Critical Facts & Details

From Service-Offerings.md:
- Principal-led discovery review (not templated audit)
- Principal involvement in all strategic decisions
- All work led by James and Andy (not juniors)
- Accountability sits with principals, not account managers
- Business strategy before technical work
- Team training and handover post-launch (client empowerment)

From Positioning.md:
- "Two principals. Forty years combined."
- Principal-led strategic pre-work is the #1 differentiator
- Trust barrier for Burned Decision Makers is primary objection (will I be delegated to juniors?)
- Medical/YMYL specialists need accountability and transparency
- E-E-A-T signals: expertise demonstrated through specialisation, years in market, specific past roles

## Stats Budget (Each Used ONCE)

| Stat/Claim | Assigned Section | Usage Type |
|------------|-----------------|------------|
| "Forty years combined experience" | Hero | Prominent |
| "Principal-led work" | Hero + Why This Matters | Prominent (Hero), Brief reference (Why This Matters) |
| "Business strategy before technical work" | Our Approach (Pillar 1) | Prominent |
| "90+ PageSpeed scores" | Our Approach (Pillar 1 or 3) | Brief reference (featured on Homepage/Services) |
| "We don't delegate to juniors" | Why This Matters | Prominent (core differentiator) |
| "20+ years [domain expertise]" | James bio or Andy bio | Prominent (one principal) |
| "Complementary expertise" | Andy bio | Prominent (partnership signal) |
| "CMS designed for non-technical teams" | Our Approach (Pillar 1 or separate) | Brief reference |
| "Owned digital asset" | Our Approach (Pillar 4) | Prominent |

## Section Narrative Plan

| Section | Narrative Role | Opens With (Conflict) | Resolves With | Key New Evidence | Frame Element | Transition From Previous | Transition To Next |
|---------|---------------|----------------------|---------------|-----------------|---------------|-------------------------|-------------------|
| Global Header | Navigation anchor | n/a — persistent | n/a | Trust signal: CTA visible | Neutral facilitator | From homepage | Seamless to Hero |
| Hero | Trust foundation + principal introduction | Unspoken question: "Who leads the work?" (implicit doubt for Burned Decision Makers) | "Two principals. Forty years combined. We do the work." | Principal names, combined years, peer-to-peer positioning | Protagonists introducing themselves; confidence without defensiveness | n/a — first section | Transition: "Here's who we are and how we got here" |
| James Bio | Principal credibility signal + specialisation | Why should I believe James has this expertise? | Clear expertise story, years in market, specialisation domains, philosophy quote | Specific years in market, named specialisation areas, business-first philosophy | Authority as guide; James as peer expert, not salesperson | "Here's who we are..." → "James brings..." | Transition: "Andy brings complementary expertise..." |
| Andy Bio | Principal credibility signal + complementary specialisation | Why does the partnership matter? (Why not just one principal?) | Clear specialisation domains (complementary to James), years in market, technical + strategic competence | Years in market, specific specialisation areas, philosophy quote, technical credibility | Complementary authority; Andy fills capability gaps | "James brings..." → "Andy brings complementary expertise..." | Transition: "You might wonder why principal-led work changes outcomes..." |
| Why This Matters | Differentiator explanation + accountability defence | Generic question: "Does principal-led really make a difference?" | Three core reasons: (1) Strategy requires judgment, not process, (2) Accountability stops with delegation, (3) Your business deserves expertise, not junior training | Mechanism-level detail on why delegation breaks accountability, why business scale justifies principal involvement | Transparency & accountability; honesty about industry norm | "James and Andy both lead..." → "You might wonder why this matters" | Transition: "Here's how we actually work..." |
| Our Approach (Three Pillars) | Methodology overview + philosophy | How do you actually work? | Translates principal leadership into concrete working principles | Three-pillar framework: (1) Business Strategy Before Technical, (2) Accountability & Judgment, (3) Continuous Client Capability Building | Philosophy & methodology; confidence in approach | "Here's the thinking that guides our work..." | Transition: "Let's talk about what happens next..." or CTA |

## Query Vocabulary Alignment

| Search Term | Brand Term | Usage Rule |
|------------|-----------|-----------|
| "About us" | "Meet the principals" / "Two principals. Forty years combined." | Brand terminology in H1 |
| "Web design agency NZ owners" | "James and Andy" (names) | Use names directly; they ARE the brand signal |
| "Principal web designers" | "Principal-led work" | Use search term concept in content |
| "Who we are" | "Two principals" / Partnership emphasis | Brand emphasis on partnership |
| "Experience and expertise" | "Forty years combined" / Named specialisations | Specific years and domain expertise |

## Thematic Vocabulary by Section

| Section | Thematic Field | Terms to Use (3-4 minimum, with variation) |
|---------|---------------|-------------|
| Hero | Leadership & partnership | principals, leadership, partnership, accountability, guidance |
| James Bio | Strategic expertise & business thinking | strategy, positioning, business logic, market understanding, decision-making |
| Andy Bio | Technical excellence & implementation | architecture, technical, implementation, performance, systems |
| Why This Matters | Professional standards & judgment | expertise, judgment, experience, accountability, standards, investment |
| Our Approach | Transparent methodology & partnership | approach, principles, methodology, clarity, partnership, communication |

## Context Depth by Section

| Section | Depth Level | Implication for Copy |
|---------|-----------|---------------------|
| Hero | BROAD | Quick credibility marker: "Two principals. Forty years combined." + one supporting line. 60-100 words. This is the assertion; depth follows below. |
| James Bio | DEEP | Full biography treatment. 250-350 words. Years in market, specific roles, specialisation domains, philosophy quote. This is where James's credibility becomes clear and specific. |
| Andy Bio | DEEP | Parallel to James bio. 250-350 words. Years in market, complementary specialisation, philosophy quote, technical credibility. Establish why partnership matters. |
| Why This Matters | MODERATE | Three core reasons explained. 300-400 words total (100-130 per reason). Mechanism-level detail on why delegation breaks accountability. Address the Burned Decision Maker fear directly. |
| Our Approach | MODERATE | Three pillars introduced. Each 80-120 words. This is overview of philosophy; service pages provide technical depth. |
